meanings.definition: an act of removing by cleansing; ridding of sediment or other undesired elements
the act of clearing yourself (or another) from some stigma or charge

examples: Deng Xiao Ping was purged several times throughout his lifetime

meanings.definition: make pure or free from sin or guilt
rinse, clean, or empty with a liquid
eject the contents of the stomach through the mouth
examples: After drinking too much, the students vomited
The patient regurgitated the food we gave him last night

meanings.definition: excrete or evacuate (someone's bowels or body)
examples: The doctor decided that the patient must be purged

meanings.definition: serving to purge or rid of sin
